meta.description: SEA AI Upskilling Tracker — corporate and government skilling commitments across Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand, Singapore
meta.notes: Analytical flags are editorial. Total headcount figures include overlapping and unverified targets. Provider types: US Hyperscaler, Chinese Cloud, Government, Multilateral, Domestic Conglomerate. Update monthly.
changelog: 2026-04-21: Expanded to 40 entries — added Chinese cloud, Huawei, government programs, multilateral/ASEAN-wide; added provider_type and scope fields

entries.name: Bersama Malaysia
entries.source: MDEC / Microsoft ongoing program, cited retrospectively in May 2024 announcement
entries.notes: Headline figure of 1.53M cited to establish track record. Broad digital literacy rather than AI-specific. Depth and outcomes not specified.
entries.flag: Depth unspecified; retroactive framing

entries.name: AI Skilling — $2.2B commitment
entries.source: Microsoft May 2, 2024 announcement (Satya Nadella visit, KL)
entries.notes: Subset of Microsoft's ASEAN-wide 2.5M commitment disaggregated to country level. Includes youth AI fluency, NGO workers, underserved communities. 3-year framing. No outcome metrics.
entries.flag: Subset of ASEAN-wide template; no outcome metrics

entries.name: AI Odyssey (developer program)
entries.source: Microsoft May 2024 announcement
entries.notes: Subset of 300K commitment. Developers earning Microsoft credentials as AI subject matter experts. Credential-based — one of the more concrete components.

entries.name: National AI Centre of Excellence
entries.source: Microsoft May 2024 announcement — Ministry of Digital partnership
entries.notes: Vague mandate: 'drive AI adoption across key industries, ensure AI governance.' Staffing, ownership, output agenda unspecified. Risk: Microsoft enterprise adoption unit dressed as sovereignty infrastructure.
entries.flag: Mandate vague; governance unclear; hyperscaler capture of CoE layer

entries.name: Google Cloud AI Skills — Malaysia
entries.source: Google Malaysia cloud region launch 2024
entries.notes: Near-identical headline figure to Microsoft commitment. Template language replicated across SEA country announcements.
entries.flag: Template boilerplate; identical number to Microsoft MY

entries.name: NVIDIA AI Skills Malaysia (MDEC MOU)
entries.source: MDEC-NVIDIA MOU, cited in YTL/ILMU context
entries.notes: Linked to YTL AI Labs ecosystem. Certification-focused; no population-level targets disclosed. Analytically significant given ILMU-Nemo dependency on NVIDIA NeMo framework.

entries.name: AWS re/Start & Cloud Skills Malaysia
entries.source: AWS Malaysia cloud region launch 2023-24
entries.notes: AWS re/Start targets unemployed/underemployed. Job-placement linked — one of few programs with outcome accountability. Targeted rather than mass headcount.
entries.flag: Relatively substantive; job-placement linked

entries.name: AI Skilling — $1.7B commitment
entries.source: Microsoft April 2024 announcement (Satya Nadella visit, Jakarta)
entries.notes: Announced 2 weeks before Malaysia. Largest country-level target in the region. Subset of ASEAN 2.5M commitment. Identical structural template to Malaysia announcement.
entries.flag: Same template as MY; subset of ASEAN-wide commitment

entries.name: SMK Coding Initiative (vocational)
entries.source: Microsoft Indonesia announcement 2024
entries.notes: Partnership with Ministry of Education targeting SMK vocational high schools. More institutionally embedded than headline skilling programs.

entries.name: Google Career Certificates Indonesia
entries.source: Google Indonesia digital skills program
entries.notes: Workforce-oriented certification. Emphasis on employability in digital jobs rather than AI capability per se.

entries.name: GPU Merdeka / NVIDIA NeMo Skills
entries.source: Lintasarta / Indosat GPU Merdeka launch, Aug 2024
entries.notes: Developer-focused; linked to Sahabat AI LLM training. More technically substantive than broad population skilling — aimed at researchers and AI engineers on NVIDIA infrastructure.
entries.flag: Linked to LLM development; more substantive than headcount programs

entries.name: AWS Skills to Jobs — Indonesia
entries.source: AWS Indonesia program
entries.notes: Certification pathways with employment focus. AWS re/Start extended to Indonesia.

entries.name: AI Skilling — $1B+ commitment
entries.source: Microsoft March 31, 2026 Thailand announcement (Brad Smith visit)
entries.notes: Third major SEA country announcement. Identical 'Technology / Trust / Talent' framework to Malaysia and Indonesia. Gulf Development and CP Group as local ecosystem partners.
entries.flag: Template boilerplate — identical structure to MY/ID

entries.name: Microsoft Elevate for Educators / Changemakers
entries.source: Microsoft Thailand March 2026 announcement
entries.notes: Education sector skilling component. Partners include Gulf Development and CP Group as ecosystem conduits — domestic conglomerates integrated into delivery.

entries.name: Google Cloud Thailand Skilling
entries.source: Google Cloud Bangkok region launch, Jan 2024
entries.notes: Economic impact framing ('$40B economic value over 5 years') rather than specific skilling targets — weakest disclosure of any Google SEA commitment.
entries.flag: No specific skilling numbers; economic impact framing only

entries.name: AWS Thailand Digital Skills (DEPA partnership)
entries.source: AWS Thailand announcements 2023
entries.notes: Part of AWS regional skills program. depa as state partner.

entries.name: Google AI Trailblazers SG
entries.source: Google Singapore program
entries.notes: Enterprise-focused AI adoption. Singapore programs more substantive — higher baseline capability and stronger accountability norms.

entries.name: Microsoft AI Singapore Partnership (IMDA)
entries.source: IMDA-Microsoft MOU 2024
entries.notes: IMDA as counterpart rather than MDEC-equivalent — higher institutional quality. Singapore operates as Microsoft's ASEAN capability and governance anchor.

entries.name: AWS re/Start Singapore
entries.source: AWS Singapore ongoing
entries.notes: Longest-running AWS skilling program in region. Job-placement outcomes tracked — benchmark for what outcome accountability looks like.
entries.flag: Benchmark: outcome-linked

entries.name: NVIDIA AI Singapore Research / NUS Partnership
entries.source: NVIDIA Singapore academic partnerships
entries.notes: Research-oriented; includes SEA-LION LLM project. Represents genuine technical capability investment vs. headcount-based programs.
entries.flag: Research depth; linked to SEA-LION

entries.name: Microsoft ASEAN: 2.5 Million AI Skills
entries.source: Microsoft announcement April 30, 2024 — one day before Malaysia $2.2B deal; ZDNet, Digital News Asia coverage
entries.notes: The parent commitment from which all country-level Microsoft skilling numbers are derived: MY (300K) and ID (840K) are disaggregated subsets. Confirms template theory: one regional announcement repackaged as country-level press events. No ASEAN-level accountability mechanism.
entries.flag: Country numbers are subsets — aggregating them double-counts; template confirmed

entries.name: AI Ready ASEAN
entries.source: ASEAN Foundation; USD 5M grant from Google.org; launched 2024; 2.5-year program; Vero-ASEAN Aug 2025
entries.notes: Targets 5.5M individuals across all 10 ASEAN states. Includes Training-of-Trainers: 2,000 master trainers cascading to 800K community members. 5-phase structure. Google.org funding means Google indirectly claims credit for a multilateral program. Cascade model quality critical and unverified.
entries.flag: Google.org funded — Google claims indirect credit; cascade quality unverified

entries.name: Alibaba Cloud Digital Accelerator Programme (SMEs)
entries.source: Ministry of Digital Malaysia endorsement, Aug 1, 2024
entries.notes: SME-focused cloud and AI adoption program endorsed by Minister of Digital at launch. Primarily a customer acquisition vehicle — helps SMEs adopt Alibaba Cloud services rather than building independent AI capability.
entries.flag: SME adoption vehicle; commercial interest dominant over capability building

entries.name: Alibaba Cloud × MDEC MOU
entries.source: Alibaba Cloud AI Day Malaysia; MDEC MOU exchange Nov 2025; joint awareness campaigns KL then nationwide; The Edge Malaysia Nov 20, 2025
entries.notes: Formal MOU with MDEC for joint SME awareness campaigns nationwide. Pattern mirrors Microsoft/Google: MOU with MDEC as state legitimation of commercial expansion.
entries.flag: State legitimation of market entry; MDEC as co-promoter

entries.name: Huawei ICT Academy Malaysia
entries.source: Huawei ICT Academy ongoing; Huawei Cloud AI Ecosystem Summit APAC, Kuala Lumpur, Aug 13, 2025
entries.notes: 22 technical fields, Huawei-certified instructors, 200+ courses. Embedded in Malaysian universities. Technically substantive (vendor certification) but proprietary to Huawei ecosystem — deepens dependency on Chinese tech stack. Analytically significant for sovereignty discourse.
entries.flag: Technically substantive but deepens Chinese vendor dependency

entries.name: Huawei Seeds for the Future — Malaysia
entries.source: Huawei annual program, ongoing across SEA
entries.notes: Selects top university students for intensive ICT program including visit to Huawei HQ in Shenzhen. Soft power / talent pipeline function as much as skills development. Annual cadence.
entries.flag: Soft power / talent pipeline; Shenzhen visit component

entries.name: GoTo × Tencent Cloud × Alibaba Cloud Digital Talent
entries.source: GoTo press release Nov 11, 2024; Business Times Nov 10, 2024; TechNode Global Nov 12, 2024
entries.notes: Three-way agreement: Indonesian tech conglomerate GoTo + Tencent Cloud + Alibaba Cloud. Cloud infrastructure + digital talent development combined. First significant Chinese cloud skilling play in Indonesia involving a major domestic tech firm. Skilling component details not publicly specified.
entries.flag: Chinese cloud entry via domestic tech firm; skilling details vague

entries.name: Alibaba Cloud Indonesia AI Conference / Model Studio
entries.source: Alibaba Cloud Indonesia AI Conference, Jakarta, Aug 13, 2025; Alibaba Cloud press room
entries.notes: Global launch of 'Model Studio: Exclusive' in Indonesia. Developer ecosystem play rather than broad skilling. Positions Indonesia as launch market for Alibaba Cloud AI products.
entries.flag: Product launch dressed as skilling; market development primary

entries.name: Huawei Cloud Summit Indonesia / ICT Academy ID
entries.source: Huawei Cloud Summit Indonesia, Jakarta, Dec 2024 (500+ representatives from government, enterprises, ecosystem)
entries.notes: Annual summit with embedded ICT Academy certification pathway. Government and enterprise focus. Technical certification into Huawei cloud ecosystem.
entries.flag: Government engagement + certification; ecosystem lock-in dynamic

entries.name: Eye for Thailand (Alibaba Cloud × depa)
entries.source: Alibaba Cloud press release May 14-15, 2025; depa partnership; Alibaba Cloud blog
entries.notes: Nationwide AI video creation challenge using Alibaba's Wan2.1 video generation model. Creative/awareness program rather than substantive skills transfer. Positions Alibaba AI tools in Thai content creator market. depa as government legitimation.
entries.flag: Awareness / product marketing; minimal skills depth; depa as legitimation vehicle

entries.name: Huawei ICT Academy Thailand / Seeds for Future TH
entries.source: Huawei Thailand ongoing; ICT Competition national round 2024-25
entries.notes: Thai national round of Huawei ICT Competition plus ongoing academy. Technically embedded in Thai universities. Part of Huawei's broader APAC talent ecosystem.

entries.name: Huawei ICT Competition 2024-25 (AI Track)
entries.source: Huawei press release May 26, 2025: '9th edition, 210,000+ students and instructors from 2,000+ institutions globally; AI track'
entries.notes: Record scale globally. All four SEA countries participate in national rounds. AI-specific track introduced this edition. 210K is global participation figure — SEA subset much smaller. Competition model means only finalists receive substantive development.
entries.flag: Global figure — SEA subset much smaller; competition model limits depth for most participants

entries.name: Huawei ICT Academy Singapore
entries.source: Huawei Singapore; university partnerships NTU, NUS
entries.notes: Singapore Huawei programs more technically advanced given higher baseline. University-embedded. Geopolitically notable: Huawei maintaining Singapore presence amid US export control pressure on Chinese tech.
entries.flag: Geopolitically notable: Huawei maintaining SG presence amid US pressure

entries.name: Kartu Prakerja (Prakerja Program)
entries.source: Prakerja LinkedIn 2023: '17M beneficiaries 2020-2023, 86% first-time formal training access'; ongoing Wave 46 active 2025; IDR 4.2M per participant (2024 scheme)
entries.notes: By far the largest skilling program in SEA by headcount — dwarfs all corporate announcements combined. National voucher-based platform funding online courses including digital and AI tracks. 86% of recipients had never accessed formal training before. Post-training employment and income surveys conducted. Analytically critical: state-led program at scale with outcome tracking that corporate programs lack entirely.
entries.flag: Benchmark: largest program in SEA by far; government-run; outcome surveys exist; makes corporate numbers look trivial

entries.name: SkillsFuture AI Courses
entries.source: Straits Times Feb 3, 2026: '105,000 people took 137,000 AI training places in 2025'; Minister Janil Puthucheary statement
entries.notes: Most rigorously tracked government skilling program in SEA. 137,000 training places taken by 105,000 people in 2025 alone (many took multiple courses). Actual completions, not targets. Course quality moderated by SSG. Benchmark for what accountability looks like vs. corporate headline commitments.
entries.flag: Benchmark: actual completions not targets; SSG quality-moderated; best accountability model in SEA

entries.name: AI-Ready SG
entries.source: NTUC launch Feb 13, 2026; CNA coverage; NTUC UPortal
entries.notes: Structured AI learning pathways for workers at all skill levels. NTUC (labor federation) + SSG (government agency) joint initiative — institutionally embedded unlike corporate programs. Emphasizes application of AI in work contexts not just literacy.
entries.flag: Institutionally embedded; application-focused rather than awareness

entries.name: MDEC AI Skills Training (HRD Corp SBL)
entries.source: MDEC website; HRD Corp SBL-claimable approval June 3, 2025; Facebook posts 2025
entries.notes: MDEC running its own AI training independent of hyperscaler co-branding. HRD Corp SBL scheme means employer-funded via Malaysia's Human Resources Development levy. More sustainable funding model than hyperscaler CSR budgets. Includes AI in Office Management and other applied courses.
entries.flag: State-run; employer-funded levy model; analytically distinct from hyperscaler programs

entries.name: Thailand Digital Skill Roadmap 2025
entries.source: depa Digital Skill Roadmap 2025; UNESCAP Thailand Digital Transformation documentation
entries.notes: National framework for digital workforce development with AI as specific track. Part of Digital Economy Promotion Master Plan. Includes Digital Catalog and Thailand Digital Valley program. Planning document rather than executed program — implementation quality TBD. depa also serves as government partner for both AWS and Alibaba Cloud SEA programs.
entries.flag: Planning framework; depa simultaneously state program manager and corporate program legitimator

entries.name: GPU Merdeka AI Training Ecosystem
entries.source: Indosat/Lintasarta GPU Merdeka launch; linked to Sahabat AI LLM; Data Center Dynamics Aug 2024
entries.notes: Domestic conglomerate (Indosat, majority-owned by Ooredoo-CK Hutchison) running AI developer training on 'sovereign' GPU infrastructure. Linked to Sahabat AI continued pre-training. Most technically substantive domestic-led program in Indonesia. Sovereignty branding ('Merdeka' = independence/freedom) while entirely dependent on NVIDIA H100s — emblematic of subordinate integration dynamic.
entries.flag: Most technically substantive ID domestic program; sovereignty branding despite NVIDIA dependency — core paper example

entries.name: ILMU AI Skilling / YTL-NVIDIA Partnership
entries.source: YTL AI Labs; ILMU 1.0 launch Aug 2025 co-promoted with MDEC; ILMU-Nemo-30B March 2026 NVIDIA co-brand
entries.notes: YTL's skilling commitments bundled with ILMU model launches. Analytically significant: skilling claims wrapped in sovereignty narrative. ILMU-Nemo-30B pivot to Mistral base model undermines sovereignty framing but skilling claims continue unchanged. MDEC as state co-promoter throughout both phases.
entries.flag: Sovereignty skilling claims persist despite technical dependency pivot — key paper evidence
